title: Quiz: Trapped Ion Gates
description: Test your understanding of trapped ion gate mechanisms

questions.question: What is a key advantage of the Molmer-Sorensen gate over the Cirac-Zoller gate?

options.text: It works at finite temperature (doesn't require ground state cooling)
It only works on two ions

questions.explanation: The MS gate uses a geometric phase mechanism that returns the motion to its starting state regardless of initial thermal occupation. This makes it robust to finite temperature.

questions.question: In the Molmer-Sorensen gate, what frequencies are applied to the ions?

options.text: Both red and blue sidebands symmetrically detuned

questions.explanation: The MS gate uses bichromatic light with frequencies symmetrically detuned from both the red and blue motional sidebands, creating a state-dependent force.

questions.question: Single-qubit gates on trapped ions typically have higher fidelity than two-qubit gates.
questions.explanation: True! Single-qubit gates achieve >99.99% fidelity while two-qubit gates are typically 99.5-99.9%. This is because two-qubit gates involve motional modes which add error sources.

questions.question: What type of entangling operation does the Molmer-Sorensen gate naturally implement?
questions.explanation: The MS gate naturally generates exp(-i*theta*sigma_x*sigma_x), an XX interaction. Other gates like CNOT can be constructed from this plus single-qubit rotations.
